top_signals.title: Stocks Gain as Buyers Snap Up Bargains; Oil Wavers: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Bargain hunters are pushing stocks higher at the start of a week in which leaders of the biggest central banks and a busy data calendar will set the tone. Oil fluctuated after a weekend of tension in the Middle East.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Alphabet burnishes one of its best weapons in the battle for AI supremacy
top_signals.summary: The Google parent's homegrown silicon is a big advantage in the AI compute race.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: The Options Trader Who Emerged as a Surprise Front-Runner to Succeed Jamie Dimon - WSJ
top_signals.summary: The Options Trader Who Emerged as a Surprise Front-Runner to Succeed Jamie Dimon  WSJ
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Mercedes-Benz slammed for swerving payout for car with ‘serious safety risk’
top_signals.summary: Mercedes-Benz’s finance arm has been slammed by the UK’s banks ombudsman for “very poor” handling of a motor finance dispute where a consumer’s car showed “serious” electrical faults.  The financial services arm of the German carmaker has been ordered to refund with interest 50 per cent of all monthly payments made by a customer after [...]
